title: Eve SOP
subtitle: Repo-wide methodology: how Eve stays grounded, run-ready, and improvable.

text.laymen: Prefer the simplest path that works. Reduce friction, but never break safety.
text.technical: Minimize unnecessary work and moving parts. Avoid auto-running expensive checks; require explicit intent for side effects.

text.laymen: Green/red status must reflect real sensors, not vibes.
text.technical: Every dashboard state must map to a concrete endpoint, file, or contract. No fake intelligence.

text.laymen: Vision and procedure are readable without code literacy.
text.technical: Technical view exposes commands, evidence paths, and raw links only with explicit intent.

text.laymen: If a gate is red, you do not let Eve run real-world actions.
text.technical: Acceptance bundles are the go/no-go barrier. When a gate fails, fix the first failure and rerun.

goal.laymen: Keep Eve grounded in reality and role-separated for safe operation.
goal.technical: Ensure policy/identity is canonical and constraints propagate via symlink invariants.

why.laymen: If the rules drift, the whole system becomes untrustworthy.
why.technical: Constraint drift increases hallucination risk and breaks repeatability across workspaces.

output.laymen: You can trust what green/red means.
output.technical: Policy symlinks and repo hygiene invariants are satisfied.

goal.laymen: Confirm the machine and wiring are real before you trust outputs.
goal.technical: Ensure required env/config exists and secrets are not hardcoded into workflows.

why.laymen: Most failures are wiring failures, not logic failures.
why.technical: Missing env/config and secret leakage are the highest-frequency and highest-risk failure modes.

output.laymen: The system is connected and safe to test.
output.technical: Preflight checks and secret-binding policy pass.

steps.title: Run strict preflight and validate runtime config

goal.laymen: Prove the system is run-ready before executing real-world actions.
goal.technical: Run acceptance bundles appropriate to the risk and environment; preserve last-green evidence.

why.technical: Bundles encode different confidence levels; last-green baselines prevent silent regressions.

output.laymen: A clear green/red signal for go/no-go.
output.technical: Acceptance evidence exists in the state dir, and failures are actionable.

steps.title: Run smoke / patched prod / ops baseline gates as needed

goal.laymen: Operate Eve with clear signals: run, observe, fix, rerun.
goal.technical: Use the dashboard to run checks; use runbooks for deployments and operational probes.

why.laymen: The system improves by addressing the first real failure.
why.technical: Fast feedback loops reduce risk; avoid changing multiple subsystems without gates.

output.technical: Validated runtime + workflow behavior under acceptance constraints.

steps.title: Use the dashboard as the operator console

goal.laymen: Prove what happened using durable evidence, not memory.
goal.technical: Ensure transcript integrity and schema migrations remain deterministic and auditable.

why.laymen: You can’t improve what you can’t verify.
why.technical: Truth tables (DB) and immutable logs reduce ambiguity and adverse side effects.

output.technical: Transcript proof and schema sources of truth are verified.

steps.title: Verify transcript proof and schema sources of truth

goal.laymen: Make the smallest change that moves the system forward, then prove it.
goal.technical: Change one subsystem at a time, run the relevant gates, and add regression tests when fixing bugs.

why.laymen: Big changes are hard to debug and risky.
why.technical: Regression tests prevent repeats; gates ensure run readiness before autonomy.

output.technical: Verified change with evidence and a test to prevent recurrence.

steps.title: When fixing a bug, add a regression test when it fits

failure_modes.laymen: Rules drift and you can’t trust what Eve will do.
failure_modes.technical: Constraint/policy drift; templates no longer reference the canonical substrate; increased hallucination risk.

failure_modes.laymen: Eve can’t do the thing you expect (missing capability).
failure_modes.technical: Connector/tool mapping missing or miswired; skills registry drifts from implementation.

failure_modes.laymen: The engine fails, stalls, or behaves unpredictably.
failure_modes.technical: Runtime modules break orchestration, task execution, or guardrails; acceptance contracts fail.

failure_modes.laymen: The system can’t connect to the real world safely.
failure_modes.technical: Missing env/config; secrets accidentally committed; workflow bindings broken.

failure_modes.laymen: Automations do the wrong thing or stop working.
failure_modes.technical: Workflow contract drift; hardcoded secrets; deployment scripts out of sync with workflow JSON.

failure_modes.laymen: Eve’s references are stale or wrong.
failure_modes.technical: Ingestion/provenance drift; non-deterministic updates; untracked sources.

failure_modes.laymen: You lose the go/no-go safety barrier.
failure_modes.technical: Acceptance runner and contracts no longer represent reality; false greens or noisy reds.
Schema mismatch, migration drift, missing fields for durability; transcript capture breaks.

failure_modes.laymen: Eve isn’t online when you need her.
failure_modes.technical: Process supervision broken (launchd/pm2), docker wiring broken, operational probes fail.
